title: Boston Police Officer Nicholas O’Malley pleads not guilty to manslaughter in deadly Roxbury shooting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston Police Officer Nicholas O'Malley pleaded not guilty to manslaughter in the fatal shooting of suspect Stephenson King Jr.
The incident occurred during a traffic stop in Roxbury, where O'Malley shot King, whom prosecutors claim was attempting to flee.
O'Malley was released on personal recognizance; further court appearance scheduled for July 23 amid calls for video evidence release.

title: Embrace Boston to open a new civic space in Downtown Crossing
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Embrace Boston to transform a Downtown Crossing property into a 35,000-square-foot civic center at 33-41 West St.
The new civic space will offer rotating art exhibitions, a media space, and community organization areas.
Embrace Boston aims to open the center by 2030, backed by donations and support from Eastern Bank and law firms.

title: Man allegedly points gun during road rage incident in Bellingham
author: John Waller
bullets: Stephen Fahey, 61, allegedly pointed a gun during a road rage incident in Bellingham.
Fahey faces charges of assault with a dangerous weapon, carrying a firearm intoxicated, and disturbing the peace.
Fahey was arrested at the 99 Restaurant parking lot after police identified his vehicle.

title: A 21-year-old accessed a lake limited to Hopkinton residents. A 70-year-old allegedly confronted him and is now charged with attempted murder.
author: John Waller
bullets: Steven Dana, 70, allegedly attempted to drown a 21-year-old using crutches at Lake Maspenock in Hopkinton.
Dana faces charges including attempted murder, strangulation/suffocation, and assault on a disabled person.
Authorities emphasize contacting police for bylaw disputes after this violent altercation at Sandy Beach.

title: Review & setlist: With Ben Folds, Boston Pops proves it’s capable of anything
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Boston Pops Orchestra accompanied winners of the 2026 Fidelity Investments Young Artists Competition at Symphony Hall.
High school senior Ethan Liu performed an original composition, 'Return to 老家 [Hometown]', with the orchestra.
Ben Folds performed a mix of piano pop tunes with orchestral backing, emphasizing the Pops' versatility.

title: Waterfront Quincy house with Boston skyline views — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: The house is a 2,256-square-foot Colonial built in 1930, located on the Quincy waterfront.
It features four bedrooms, two full bathrooms, and offers views of the Boston skyline.
The annual property taxes are $9,313, and the potential listing prices range from $1.25 million to $1.45 million.

title: Karen Read unleashes tidal wave of explicit texts between Proctor and Goode in new lawsuit
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Karen Read sues Canton and Massachusetts State Police, alleging civil conspiracy and negligent hiring, training, and supervision.
The lawsuit includes explicit texts between ex-Trooper Michael Proctor and former Canton Police Sgt. Sean Goode.
Proctor and Goode's messages are claimed to reveal entrenched bigotry and hatred towards various minority groups.

title: Fans welcomed Cape Verde’s ‘Blue Sharks’ at Logan Airport ahead of the World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Cape Verde, one of the smallest nations to qualify for a World Cup, received a warm welcome at Logan Airport.
The team will play a friendly against Bermuda in Hartford on June 6 before starting World Cup matches.
Cape Verde's World Cup journey starts June 15 against Spain in Atlanta, followed by matches with Uruguay and Saudi Arabia.

title: What Boston meteorologists are saying about ‘summer-like warmth’ into the weekend
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Boston experiences summer-like warmth with temperatures reaching 80s to 90°F, less humid conditions expected.
Northern New England may witness visible northern lights on June 4-5, meteorologist says.
Weather forecast predicts three consecutive days near 90°F, impacting Boston into Saturday.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (June 4, 2026)
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Bey opens in South End, offering Lebanese dishes like beef pita pockets and hummus with beef tenderloin.
Buitoni Spaghetti Bar set to open in Kenmore Square in 2027, featuring fresh pasta and casual fare.
Kowloon Tiki opens at Revere Beach, serving Chinese-American cuisine and tropical-themed cocktails.
